role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for scheduling the clinical wound care training and confirming the home health agency acceptance, while the Navigator focuses on auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Medical Assistant's first-day role, and documenting caregiver sentiment regarding their readiness to manage the diabetic wound.
narrative_summary: Elena, a 69-year-old patient recovering from a complex diabetic foot ulcer, faced a significant hurdle when her daughter expressed overwhelming anxiety about managing sterile dressing changes at home. The Navigator identified this lack of confidence during a routine bedside visit and documented the family's distress in Atlantis, ensuring the record reflected the barrier to a safe transition. By directing the family to request a formal clinical demonstration from the Social Worker and the nursing staff, the Navigator helped facilitate the necessary training that allowed the family to feel prepared for the upcoming discharge.
demographic_audit_note: I performed a thorough audit of the face sheet against the Atlantis profile and discovered that the patient's primary contact number had two digits transposed; I corrected this in Atlantis to ensure the home team can reach the daughter for the initial visit coordination.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with specialized wound care and home health nursing.
v_card_flyer_status: I provided the Healing Partners program flyer to the daughter and successfully helped her save the V-Card into her phone contacts so she would recognize our caller ID when the Medical Assistant calls to coordinate the first home visit.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I reached the daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; she confirmed that the facility nurse had finally completed the hands-on wound care training and she felt much more confident about the transition tomorrow morning.
atlantis_final_sync: I updated Atlantis with the confirmed Thursday discharge date and scheduled the Medical Assistant's first home visit for Friday morning to ensure the 24-hour post-discharge window was met.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The daughter informed me during a bedside visit that she is terrified of changing her mother's diabetic wound dressings because she has not received any instruction from the facility staff; I documented this sentiment in Atlantis for my records and suggested she speak with the Social Worker immediately.

I asked the Social Worker for a status update on the discharge plan, and she confirmed that the transition is delayed until the family completes a mandatory competency check for wound care; I recorded this update in the Atlantis timeline and continued to monitor the situation.

The Social Worker called to confirm that the daughter passed her training and the home health agency has officially accepted the referral for a Thursday start; I documented the agency name and the discharge date in Atlantis and began the final transition preparations with the family.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter expressed significant fear about managing the patient's insulin and wound care without professional help.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented the family's high anxiety and lack of clinical training in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the need for a bedside teaching session.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's address in Atlantis was missing the apartment number found on the facility face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated the demographic records in Atlantis to include the full apartment details to prevent any delays for the Medical Assistant's arrival.

format_2_triples.situation: The family was unclear about the difference between the home health nurse and our Medical Assistant.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the Healing Partners program, specifically explaining that the Medical Assistant provides supplemental support within 24 hours of discharge.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notices that the Social Worker has not yet provided an update on the home health agency's acceptance and chooses to document the communication gap in Atlantis while waiting for a return call.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's role as the owner of the discharge timeline and prevents the Navigator from overstepping into vendor management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the caregiver training and then conducts a pulse call to the daughter to confirm her sentiment about the upcoming home transition.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This gathers necessary information for the Navigator's own documentation and provides support to the family within the Navigator's professional lane.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency intake department to ask if they can send a nurse who is also a certified diabetes educator to provide extra training for the daughter.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because it violates the no-vendor rule and involves the Navigator in clinical specialty requests and agency coordination.
